title: WebGPU draw-boundary pixel probes read the previous completed frame
suspect: src/platform/fast3d/gfx_webgpu.c:WebGPU readback during an open render pass

history.note: A 2,156-row all-triangle WebGPU probe reported zero changes because read_framebuffer_rgb sampled s_present_target_tex from the previous completed frame; Metal already flushes and resumes its live encoder for this diagnostic contract.

wgpu_flush only records commands and the scene encoder is submitted at end_frame, so synchronous mid-frame readback cannot observe the selected draw without a partial submit; selecting s_present_target_tex also points at the prior presented frame.

Implement WebGPU parity with Metal's established contract: end/submit the live pass, resume color+depth with Load semantics, and read s_scene_tex; preserve normal one-submit gameplay behavior when no diagnostic readback occurs.

WebGPU now partially submits only when readback occurs during an open frame, uploads the deferred VBO first, reads the live scene texture, and resumes color/depth with Load+Store; focused Dam probes recover real draw transitions while the probe-armed and unarmed screenshots remain byte-identical.

Tier 1-2 validation exercised all gates: focused guard PASS; 57/58 port tests PASS, renderer parity/surface XLU/perf/gate routes/sim invariance/uncap purity all PASS. The sole port failure is the independently reproducible FID-0046 Streets hash regression with byte-identical screenshots and no readback call; the other red item was the expected pre-render stale ledger index. The FID-0145 diagnostic contract is verified and ordinary gameplay remains outside the slow path.
